keyword: best ai tools for graduate students

serp.note: 10 tools, organized by Benefits/Features/Limitations/Costs; strong on faculty-approval + hallucination cautions. Gap: not stage-based, no quick stack.
~3500w, stage-based (discovery/mapping/analysis/writing), pricing table, ethics H2, 6-Q FAQ. Strong baseline to beat.
~5400w, 12 tools, persona recs, integrity FAQ. Generic 'students', not grad-specific.
5 categories, postgrad-focused, but NO pricing and shallow — our differentiation: pricing realism + depth.
10 tools, comparison table w/ pricing+limits, FAQ on scanners/cheating. Gap: not grad-tailored, no free-only stack.
Authoritative integrity guidance (UT Austin honor code), Gemini/Copilot/NotebookLM. Institutional angle.
study-tool ranking, not opened (sufficient sample reached)
free-tools angle, covered by our free-stack section
free-tools listicle, redundant with opened sources

factcheck.claim: Perplexity Pro is ~$20/mo; verified-student Education plan is ~$10/mo
Elicit has a free tier (100M+ papers) with Pro around $49/mo for high volume
NotebookLM is free and answers only from user-uploaded sources with page citations; Otter.ai free tier caps monthly minutes
Claude and ChatGPT Pro/Plus are ~$20/mo each
Consensus has free searches with a paid plan in the low-teens/mo (sources differ $10-$15, so hedged)

variables.keyword: best ai tools for graduate students
variables.headline_finding: Competitors target generic 'students'; few are tailored to grad-specific jobs (thesis, quals, multi-year lit review) — our differentiation angle.
variables.differentiator_1: Stage-based grad workflow (read/notes/write/analyze) + free-only stack on a stipend budget
variables.differentiator_2: Pricing realism + student discounts (Perplexity Education ~$10) where competitors omit pricing
